topic: Handcrafted image features (HOG) and their limits
key_concepts: Before deep learning, image processing relied heavily on handcrafted features (representasi yang dirancang manusia) to extract structural information like edges, corners, and textures from raw pixels.

Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG) is a prime example of a designed feature. It works by counting the occurrences of gradient orientation in localized portions of an image, capturing object shapes and silhouettes effectively.

The limitations of the designed approach: handcrafted extractors are rigid, require significant domain expertise to tune, and struggle to capture high-level semantic meaning (e.g., distinguishing complex contexts).
This establishes the 'designed by human' end of the spectrum for computer vision, highlighting why the field eventually shifted toward learned representations.

definitions.definition: A feature descriptor used in computer vision for object detection, focusing on the structure or the shape of an object through gradient directions.
A directional change in the intensity or color in an image, used to detect edges.

examples: Explain how HOG processes a picture of a pedestrian by looking at the sharp intensity changes (edges) around the human silhouette, rather than memorizing the color of their shirt.

relations.builds_toward: ch12_s03 (how CNNs automate the feature extraction process that HOG used to do manually)

exclusions: Do not provide the mathematical formula for calculating HOG gradients or block normalization.
Do not discuss SIFT or SURF in detail, stick to HOG as the primary example.

figures.caption: Ekstraksi fitur Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG) pada citra.
figures.description: Menampilkan gambar asli, kemudian panah gradien yang mendeteksi tepian, dan representasi akhir blok HOG yang menangkap bentuk siluet objek.
